title: Control-loop runner: unbounded shell loop driving a cold headless claude per iteration
action: Write the runner that makes an unbounded control loop possible, per the chosen mechanism in approach.md. Each iteration invokes a FRESH headless claude (`claude -p <prompt> --model sonnet`) and then sleeps; no --continue and no --resume, because the cold start per iteration is what keeps the context profile flat over thousands of iterations (approach.md, 'Why the cold start is the point'). Flags: --role dispatch|merge (selects which prompt is sent), --interval N (seconds, default 60, per D4), --max-iterations N (test-only bound; production runs unbounded per D19), --command CMD (test-only override that replaces the claude invocation so the loop can be verified without burning tokens), --once. The loop MUST survive a non-zero exit from the iteration command: report it and continue to the next sleep. There must be no code path in which a failed iteration terminates the loop (D19) - achieve this structurally in the shell, never by asking the agent to remember it. Write byte-identical copies into both managed skill roots. Use .bee/tmp/ for any scratch file.

truths: A failing iteration command does not end the loop; the configured number of iterations still runs
No --continue or --resume flag appears in the claude invocation
The two copies under .claude/ and .agents/ are byte-identical

artifacts.substantive: unbounded poll-act loop with role, interval, and test-injection flags

prohibitions: Do not add --continue/--resume; that would reintroduce unbounded context growth the approach rejected
Do not write any file under docs/history/
Do not touch src/ or web/

verify: rm -f .bee/tmp/apo-loop-selftest.log && mkdir -p .bee/tmp && bash .claude/skills/bee-orchestrating/scripts/control-loop.sh --role dispatch --interval 1 --max-iterations 3 --command "sh -c 'echo x >> .bee/tmp/apo-loop-selftest.log; exit 1'" ; test "$(wc -l < .bee/tmp/apo-loop-selftest.log)" = 3 && diff -q .claude/skills/bee-orchestrating/scripts/control-loop.sh .agents/skills/bee-orchestrating/scripts/control-loop.sh && echo LOOP_SURVIVES_FAILURE
